data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM GDP 

Gross Domestic Product represents the total value of the country's production during the period and consists of the purchases of domestically produced goods and services by individuals, businesses, foreigners, and government entities.

The initial estimate for Q4 is seen at a growth rate of 2.8 percent after 4.4 percent in Q3. PCE is expected at 2.4 percent after 3.5 percent in Q3. 

8:30 AM Personal Income and Outlays

Personal income represents the income that households receive from all sources, including wages and salaries, fringe benefits such as employer contributions to private pension plans, proprietors' income, income from rent, dividends and interest, and transfer payments such as Social Security and unemployment compensation. 

Spending is expected to be up 0.3 percent and income at 0.3 percent in the delayed report for December. Core PCE prices are expected to be up 0.3 percent, an unpleasant outcome for anyone hoping for more aggressive rate cuts.

9:45 AM PMI Composite Flash

The flash Composite Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) provides an early estimate of current private sector output by combining information obtained from surveys of around 1,000 manufacturing and service sector companies.

Manufacturing PMI is seen at 51.9 in February flash versus 52.4 in the January final. 

9:45 AM Raphael Bostic Speaks

Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic participates in a moderated conversation on the economic outlook before the event hosted by the Birmingham Business Journal. 

10:00 AM New Home Sales

New home sales measure the number of newly constructed homes with a committed sale during the month.

The delayed reports are expected to show sales at an annual 735K rate in November and 728K rate in December.

10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment

The University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center questions households each month on their assessment of current conditions and expectations of future conditions.

The final February report is expected to show the sentiment index at 57.0 versus 57.3 in the preliminary February report compared with 56.4 in the January final and a much stronger 64.7 a year ago.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.

3:30 PM Alberto Musalem Speaks 

St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem appears on Fox Business.

data.title: Forum Energy beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Forum Energy press release (FET): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $202.2M (+0.6% Y/Y) beats by $11.70M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $23 million
  • Operating cash flow and free cash flow: $22 million and $22 million, respectively.
  • Orders: $891 million, book-to-bill ratio of 113%.

  • Full Year 2026 Guidance (comparisons at midpoint)

    • Revenue: $800 - $880 million, up 6% vs. consensus of $802.90M
    • Adjusted net income: $18 - $38 million, up $21 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA: $90 - $110 million, a 16% increase
    • Free cash flow: $55 - $75 million, 65% free cash flow conversion

data.title: Lundin Mining beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; sees copper production 2026 forecast to remain stable
data.description:
• Lundin Mining press release (LUNMF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.42 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $1.35B (+62.0% Y/Y) beats by $140M, comprised of $1,301.5 million from continuing operations with a realized copper price3 of $5.89 /lb and a realized gold price3 of $4,412 /oz, and $52.2 million from discontinued operations4.
  • Copper Production2: Consolidated copper production of 87,032 tonnes at a consolidated copper cash cost3 of $1.88 /lb.
  • Other Production2: During the quarter, 34,129 ounces of gold and 2,174 tonnes of nickel were produced.
  • Adjusted EBITDA3: $700.6 million for the quarter, $686.4 million generated from continuing operations and $14.2 million generated from discontinued operations.
  • Free cash flow from operations3 was $412.5 million, $388.3 million from continuing operations and $24.2 million from discontinued operations, which included a working capital build of $132.1 million from continuing operations.
  • Net cash3: As at December 31, the net cash position of the Company was $77.4 million which includes $22.0 million cash from discontinued operations.
  • Outlook: Copper production is forecast to remain stable at approximately 310,000 – 335,000 tonnes annually in 2026, consistent with 2025 production. Consolidated cash cost in 2026 is forecast to be within $1.90/lb to $2.10/lb of copper which is in line with 2025 guidance. As part of the Company's full potential programs, the focus will continue to be on cost reductions and process improvements.

data.title: Centerra Gold Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41, revenue of $401.6M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Centerra Gold press release (CGAU): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.41.
  • Revenue of $401.6M (+32.8% Y/Y).
  • In the fourth quarter 2025, consolidated gold production was 70,853 ounces, including 44,105 ounces from the Mount Milligan Mine (“Mount Milligan”) and 26,748 ounces from the Öksüt Mine (“Öksüt”). Copper production in the quarter was 13.0 million pounds.
  • Fourth quarter 2025 gold sales were 68,143 ounces at an average realized gold price of $3,415 per ounce and copper sales were 12.5 million pounds at an average realized copper price of $4.69 per pound. 
  • Fourth quarter 2025 consolidated gold production costs were $1,259 per ounce and all-in sustaining costs (“AISC”) on a by-product basisNG were $1,646 per ounce.
  •  Fourth quarter 2025 additions to property, plant, and equipment (“PP&E”) and capital expendituresNG were $115.2 million and $96.0 million, respectively.

  • 2026 Guidance Highlights

    • Production: In 2026, consolidated gold production is expected to be 250,000 to 280,000 ounces. Copper production in 2026 is expected to be 50 to 60 million pounds.
    • Costs: In 2026, consolidated gold production costs are expected to be $1,500 to $1,600 per ounce and AISC on a by-product basisNG is expected to be $1,650 to $1,750 per ounce.
    • Capital ExpendituresNG: In 2026, sustaining capital expendituresNG are expected to be $85 to $105 million.
    • Molybdenum Roasting: Following the incident that occurred at Langeloth in late January 2026, Centerra continues to assess the full operational and financial impacts, and will provide 2026 operating guidance for Langeloth at a later date.

data.title: Fairfax Financial reports FY results
data.description:
• Fairfax Financial press release (FRFHF): FY Net premiums written by the property and casualty insurance and reinsurance operations increased by 3.9% to a record $26.3 billion from $25.3 billion, while gross premiums written increased by 2.3%.
  • The consolidated undiscounted combined ratio of the property and casualty insurance and reinsurance operations was 93.0%, producing record underwriting profit of $1,816.6 million, compared to an undiscounted combined ratio of 92.7% and underwriting profit of $1,791.4 million in 2024. 
  • The consolidated statement of earnings included a net loss of $58.5 million (2024 – a net loss of $529.9 million)

data.title: Candel Therapeutics prices $100M public offering at $5.45 per share
data.description:
• Candel Therapeutics (CADL) announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 18,348,624 shares of its common stock at a price to the public of $5.45 per share.
  • The gross proceeds to Candel from the offering are expected to be $100 million, before deducting underwriting discounts and commissions and other offering expenses.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 23, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Eldorado Gold Non-GAAP EPS of $0.63 beats by $0.05, revenue of $577.2M beats by $55.49M
data.description:
• Eldorado Gold press release (EGO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.63 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $577.2M (+32.5% Y/Y) beats by $55.49M.
  • Gold production: 123,416 ounces in Q4 2025. Full-year 2025 production of 488,268 ounces, achieving the higher-end of 2025 production guidance.
  • Gold sales: 126,923 ounces in Q4 2025 at an average realized gold price per ounce sold(1) of $4,251, resulting in 491,204 ounces sold in 2025 at an average realized gold price per ounce sold of $3,505.
  • Production costs: $203.0 million in Q4 2025, and $677.6 million in 2025.
  • Total cash costs(1): $1,295 per ounce sold in Q4 2025, and $1,176 per ounce sold in 2025 came in at the low end of our tightened guidance range.
  • All-in sustaining costs(1) ("AISC"): $1,894 per ounce sold in Q4 2025 and $1,664 per ounce sold in 2025, within the tightened guidance range for the year.
  • Total capital expenditures: $309.2 million in Q4 2025.
  • Negative $54.5 million in Q4 2025, and negative $232.9 million in 2025. Free cash flow excluding capital expenditures at Skouries(2) was $109.3 million in Q4 2025 and $315.6 million in 2025.
  • Adjusted net earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization ("Adjusted EBITDA")(2): $265.2 million in Q4 2025 and $836.2 million in 2025. 
  • First production of the copper-gold concentrate is expected in early Q3 2026 and commercial production is expected in Q4 2026, with 2026 gold production projected to be between 60,000 and 100,000 ounces and copper production projected to be between 20 and 40 million pounds.

data.title: Avista raises dividend by 0.5% to $0.4925 a share
data.description:
• Avista (AVA) declares $0.4925/share quarterly dividend, 0.5% increase from prior dividend of $0.4900.
  • Forward yield 4.68%
  • Payable March 13; for shareholders of record Feb. 25; ex-div Feb. 25.
  • The company raised its quarterly dividend by 0.5% after paying a quarterly dividend of $0.49 in each of the previous 4 quarters.
  • See AVA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Trump orders U.S. government to prepare release of UFO, alien files
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Thursday said he is directing the Defense Department and other agencies to “begin the process of identifying and releasing Government files related to alien and extraterrestrial life, unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP), and unidentified flying objects (UFOs).”

He made the declaration in a post on Truth Social, after he accused former President Barack Obama earlier in the day of revealing classified information during public remarks about aliens, saying he “made a big mistake.”

"He took it out of classified information ... He's not supposed to be doing that," Trump told reporters while traveling to Georgia. Trump added that he had not seen evidence that aliens exist, saying, "I don't know if they're real or not."

During an interview with podcast host Brian Tyler Cohen released on Saturday, Obama was asked if aliens were real.

"They're real, but I haven't seen them, and they're not being kept in ... Area 51. There's no underground facility unless there's this enormous conspiracy and they hid it from the president of the United States," Obama said.

In recent years, the Pentagon has examined reports of UFOs, and senior military officials said in 2022 they found no evidence that extraterrestrials had visited or crash-landed on Earth.

data.title: LegalZoom outlines 8% revenue growth target for 2026 while expanding human-in-the-loop strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LegalZoom.com, Inc. (LZ) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Jeffrey Stibel emphasized LegalZoom's 25-year journey and its strategic pivot toward integrating AI and human expertise, stating, "LegalZoom makes it safe and seamless to finish. Since I became CEO, we have been steadily refocusing our business to capture the AI opportunity while recognizing that certain tasks and complex legal matters will always require human judgment and supervision." He highlighted the company's "human-in-the-loop" approach as the driver of growth, combining automation with expert intervention.
  • Stibel explained that the strategy is to "be the trusted guardians of small businesses and individuals lives and aspirations, enabled by the best technology available," focusing on automating where possible and leveraging human expertise for nuanced tasks. He announced the expansion beyond business formations to serve existing businesses, aiming to increase share of their serviceable addressable market through expert and service layers, including legal advice subscriptions, trademark and IP services, and concierge offerings.
  • CFO and COO Noel Watson stated, "For the full year 2025, we grew revenue 11% to $756 million, more than double the growth rate from our initial outlook, inclusive of the Formation Nation acquisition." He added, "Full year subscription revenue increased 13%... and we expect that momentum to continue in 2026."
  • Watson also highlighted a focus on higher-value subscriptions and AI-enabled services, noting, "Our strategy is focused on building a more resilient revenue base through higher value subscription offerings and AI-enabled human-in-the-loop services."

Outlook

  • LegalZoom projects full year 2026 revenue in the range of $805 million to $825 million, which Watson described as "approximately 8% year-over-year growth at the midpoint." The acceleration is expected to come from higher value offerings, quality customer acquisition, and continued execution of the human-in-the-loop strategy.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for 2026 is expected between $190 million and $200 million. Watson said, "Our outlook reflects improved gross margins and disciplined cost management, partially offset by higher product and marketing investments."
  • For Q1 2026, revenue is expected in the range of $200 million to $203 million with adjusted EBITDA of $34 million to $36 million, reflecting a temporary year-over-year decline due to front-loaded marketing investments.
  • Management stated that a recent 5% reduction in headcount was completed "allowing for improved operating leverage while preserving investment in high-growth initiatives."

Financial Results

  • LegalZoom reported Q4 2025 total revenue of $190 million, with subscription revenue reaching $131 million, marking the fourth consecutive quarter of accelerating growth.
  • Subscription units ended at approximately 1.94 million, up 10% year-over-year, with ARPU at $266 for the quarter.
  • Transaction revenue for Q4 was $59 million, with average order value at $248, up 13% year-over-year. The company processed 112,000 business formations, a 17% increase.
  • Deferred revenue declined by $10 million sequentially, attributed to normal seasonality.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $50 million, representing a margin of 26%. Free cash flow for the quarter was $28 million, with $148 million for the full year.
  • Cash and cash equivalents stood at $203 million, with share repurchases totaling approximately $80 million for the year. The Board approved a $100 million increase to the share repurchase authorization.

Q&A

  • Eleanor Smith, JPMorgan: Asked about early metrics on the concierge product and its impact on 2026 expectations. Stibel responded that "there are some early proof points and the green shoots, and we have factored those in... We're still in the early innings."
  • Smith followed up on customer acquisition marketing. Stibel indicated increased Q1 marketing spend to target high-value customers, with Watson adding, "The Q1 incremental marketing spend is driven primarily by brand... we expect CAM spend to be relatively in line from a percent of revenue standpoint for the full year."
  • Trevor Young, Barclays: Inquired about revenue growth cadence and what needs to happen for durable double-digit growth. Watson noted tougher comps due to prior year initiatives, while Stibel pointed to expanding the human-in-the-loop strategy and market penetration as essential for future acceleration.
  • Michael McGovern, BofA: Asked about the mechanics of LegalZoom's role as a last-mile provider for LLMs. Stibel said, "The bottom line is the right answer is we don't know and they don't know yet. However, both parties have identified a problem... And we want to be that solution to the now what."
  • Lucas Cerisola, Morgan Stanley: Requested details on Formation Nation's contribution in Q4. Watson confirmed, "Formation Nation contributed about $9.8 million on the transaction side and $5.7 million in subscription revenue."
  • Additional analyst questions focused on investments in human-in-the-loop services, top-of-funnel metrics, competitive dynamics, and marketing strategy, with management reiterating their emphasis on quality share, efficiency, and leveraging AI.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised questions about growth durability, the pace of innovation, and the impact of AI partnerships, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone with a focus on execution and strategy validation.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, frequently referencing "confidence," "momentum," and "strong execution," and using phrases like, "We are confident this will enable us to capture a greater share of our serviceable addressable market."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained optimistic but acknowledged the need for continued execution, while analysts continued to probe for specifics on strategy and growth sustainability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 reflects an 8% revenue growth target, up from 3% organic growth in 2025, signaling increased confidence in higher-value offerings and the human-in-the-loop approach.
  • The previous quarter highlighted new partnerships and organizational realignment, while the current quarter emphasized operational execution, margin expansion, and active share repurchases.
  • Analyst questions in both quarters focused on the evolution of subscription models, the impact of AI, and the company's ability to sustain growth amid shifting market dynamics.
  • Management's confidence in the human-in-the-loop strategy and expanding addressable market has grown, with increased emphasis on leveraging AI for both internal efficiency and customer acquisition.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited normal seasonality affecting deferred revenue and the need to balance cost discipline with ongoing investments in marketing and product.
  • Watson noted, "We continue to be disciplined with head count as our organization onboards more AI and technology into our processes."
  • Analysts voiced concerns about the pace of customer acquisition, competitive intensity, and the execution of new product rollouts, while management stressed their focus on quality share and margin improvement.

Final Takeaway

LegalZoom enters 2026 with a strengthened foundation, targeting 8% revenue growth and positioning its human-in-the-loop services and high-value subscriptions as primary engines for durable expansion. Management continues to leverage AI-driven efficiencies while prioritizing quality customer acquisition and operational discipline, supported by robust free cash flow and an expanded share repurchase authorization.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
